paper.title: Learning Rate Matters: Vanilla LoRA May Suffice for LLM Fine-tuning

reference_repo_influences: MinerU: parser option for paper source
MinerU-Document-Explorer: future multi-paper LoRA corpus index
algorithm_traits: batch-size and duration are part of comparison context
Hessian maximum eigenvalue can guide LR range
vanilla LoRA remains a strong baseline

maformac_insertion_points.recommendation: Future receipt fields for lr_grid, rank_grid, seed_count, and comparison_scope.
Future command rendering should support sweep packets rather than one learning rate.
Reuse existing metrics JSONL learning_rate and grad norm fields for future sweep ledger.
Future adapter comparison should report the LR/rank/seed grid before C6 claims.

gate.rationale: The paper directly affects how MAformac should justify LoRA training claims, but current route does not authorize training or C5 schema changes.
stop_conditions: No learning-rate change to mainline config.
No adapter quality claim from this research packet.
No C6 comparison without future OpenSpec authorization.

residual_risks: Paper tasks differ from MAformac vehicle tool-call data.
Official repo is low-star and should not be treated as community-validated.
Hessian diagnostics may be too expensive for first C5 adoption.
